mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate indicates improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer income and spending confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 6.7% to 6.1% over the past year
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative in >60% of months where UNRATE declines sequentially over two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or below 2% signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, lowering the discount rate on equities and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 1.81% as of latest observation
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative in >50% of months where FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer and housing demand, reinforcing corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth > 2% and HOUST year-over-year growth > 5%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >40% of periods where both trigger conditions hold simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, persistently rising industrial production becomes a warning of overheating and peak earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate a slowdown.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO growth appears decelerating from prior above-trend pace
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in >50% of quarters with above-trend INDPRO growth during a regime classified as late-cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Low and stable consumer price inflation reduces uncertainty about future monetary policy and discount rates, supporting equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI index is 218.88, indicating low inflation
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative in >55% of months where year-over-year CPI inflation is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly gains in nonfarm payrolls signal strong labor demand and economic momentum, providing a short-term tailwind for equity markets.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in >50% of instances where payrolls increase by more than 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FFR minus CPI inflation) is very low, monetary policy is extremely accommodative, amplifying equity demand over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are negative in >40% of quarters where the real rate is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth in a post-recession recovery confirms that the expansion is self-sustaining, raising corporate profit expectations and boosting S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is 11727.4, reflecting recovery from recession
failure_condition: 60-day returns are negative in >30% of quarters where real GDP growth exceeds 3% annualized.
